data.title: Cineplex reports september box office results
data.description:
• Cineplex (CGX:CA) announced on Thursday box office revenues of $37.7M for September 2025, led by a strong opening for the movie Conjuring: Last Rites.
  • The movie led the month with an $84M debut, marking the third-largest opening for any horror film in history. 
  • While July 2025 box office revenues were at par with July 2024, August 2025 saw a major slump compared to last year, dragging the Q3 revenues to $159.4M compared to $174.8M in the previous year.

data.title: Citizens Financial stock upgraded at BofA Securities on 25% upside potential
data.description:
Citizens Financial (NYSE:CFG) was trading higher after BofA Securities upgraded the banking stock on a 25% upside potential.

Shares were +0.96% to $52.51 before the opening bell on Thursday.

"We upgrade Citizens Financial (NYSE:CFG) to Buy from Neutral, raise PO to $65 from $52, now based on 12.5x our 2026 P/E and 1.6x our 2026 P/TBV (vs 10x/1.3x prior) implying 25% potential upside," said analysts including Ebrahim Poonawala and Brandon Berman.

"While we have missed the stock’s initial outperformance (1700bp YTD vs. super regional bank peers) we think valuation remains compelling given multiple idiosyncratic catalysts to drive EPS growth," said the research note.

The investment bank estimates over 300 basis points of improvement in the return on tangible common equity by 2028, accelerating pre-provision net revenue growth, and M&A optionality boosting stock valuation.

BofA's rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system sees the stock as Strong Buy.

data.title: Figma partners with Google Cloud to expand AI-powered design tools
data.description:
Figma (NYSE:FIG) on Thursday announced a collaboration with Google Cloud (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) to expand the use of artificial intelligence (AI) across its design and product development platform.

Google’s AI models, including Gemini 2.5 Flash, Gemini 2.0, and Imagen 4, will further help Figma's more than 13 million monthly active users' product design needs, the company said. With Gemini's context windows, AI toolset, data platform, and cybertools, Figma expects to meet the increasing demand for user-friendly AI tools.

“With Gemini on Google Cloud, Figma can more easily help its users make high-quality images with just a prompt; experiment with site functions before launch; and accelerate product development by eliminating tedious workflows that slow the design process down,” Google said in its press release.

Early integrations of Gemini 2.5 Flash helped Figma cut latency by 50% for its "Make Image" feature, delivering faster and more responsive image generation.

Earlier this week, ChatGPT CEO Sam Altman  announced the integration of Figma and other apps on its AI platform, sending the cloud-based design platform’s shares soaring by over 10%.

On Monday, during his keynote speech at DevDay 2025, Altman launched Apps SDK, which will provide access to various apps within the ChatGPT dialogue window.

data.title: Talen Energy announces launch of proposed senior notes offerings
data.description:
• Talen Energy (NASDAQ: TLN) announced on Thursday that Talen Energy Supply, a direct wholly owned subsidiary of TEC, proposed to offer and sell senior notes due 2034 and 2036 in a private offering.
  • The company plans to use the current and new net proceeds of the $1.2M senior secured term loan B credit facility to fund the previously announced acquisitions of the Freedom Energy Center and the Guernsey Power Station.
  • The notes and related guarantees will be offered exclusively to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. persons under the Securities Act.

data.title: Sealed Air upgraded by RBC Capital Markets as cost cuts, food growth drive turnaround
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets late Wednesday upgraded Sealed Air Corporation (NYSE:SEE) to Outperform from Sector Perform, citing stabilizing volumes in its packaging businesses, aggressive cost-cutting initiatives and the potential for meaningful upside in the company’s stock price.

Packaging segments show signs of recovery

Analysts led by Arun Viswanathan expect Sealed Air’s (NYSE:SEE) protective packaging division, which has struggled since 2022, to stabilize by late 2025, with modest growth in industrial and electronics demand helping to lift volumes. Meanwhile, its food segment remains resilient, buoyed by steady demand for fresh protein in both retail and foodservice channels. The firm highlighted gains in Europe and Latin America as signs of competitive momentum.

Aggressive cost savings targeted

The report points to more than $100 million in expected annual savings through Sealed Air’s (SEE) “CTO2Grow” program, which includes supply chain streamlining, reduced general and administrative expenses, and offshoring initiatives. These savings could account for as much as 15–17% of projected earnings per share in fiscal 2025 and 2026. Analysts believe additional efficiency gains may follow in the years ahead.

Valuation and upside potential

RBC raised its price target to $48 from $35, implying more than 30% upside from current levels. The analysts value Sealed Air (SEE) at nine times expected 2026 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, with the potential to reroute free cash flow to share buybacks once net leverage falls to 3 times. In a bullish scenario, shares could climb as high as $59, while downside risks could push them to $33.

Headwinds and risks

The company still faces challenges, including weaker beef consumption in the United States -- a key end-market for its food packaging segment -- and macroeconomic pressures in Europe, where it has significant exposure. Longer-term secular shifts away from red meat could also pose risks to demand.

Strategic reset under way

The analysts noted Sealed Air’s (SEE) push into automation and digitalization, with the goal of having half of its sales online by 2025. Combined with sustainability initiatives -- such as compostable trays and reduced-resin packaging -- the company is positioning itself for incremental growth of 6% to 9% over the long term.

data.title: Nvidia receives price target high of $300 due to multi-trillion AI buildout: Cantor
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) shows no signs of slowing its efforts to dominate the processing power behind the multi-trillion-dollar buildout of artificial intelligence infrastructure, prompting Cantor to reiterate its "Top Pick" Overweight rating and increase its price target to a Wall Street high of $300 from $240.

The next highest target was set by Melius Research, which increased its target to $275 from $240 earlier this week. Goldman Sachs recently increased its price target to $210 from $200. Last month, Bank of America increased its target to $235 from $220, and Barclays boosted its target to $240 from $200. In August, Wells Fargo hiked its target to $220 from $185.

During early market action on Thursday, Nvidia shares had increased nearly 2%, reaching a new intraday record high. Shares have doubled in value in the last six months.

The new target follows a series of investor meetings Cantor analysts had with Nvidia's top executives this week, including CEO Jensen Huang and CFO Colette Kress. They discussed details of the new partnership with OpenAI (OPENAI).

"The vision here is to remove margin stacking by server ODM's and CSP's, enabling the cost delta between NVDA and ASICs to only an average of 15% - truly a win-win for both and placing potentially further pressure on the ASIC market," said Cantor analysts, led by C.J. Muse, in a Thursday investor note. "The company continues to work at Extreme Co-Design at an annual cadence, enabling the optimization of the entire AI Infrastructure (no one wants to buy chips only, they want to deploy AI at scale), helped by a full-stack solution including CUDA-X."

Cantor now sees Nvidia hitting an $8 earnings per share in calendar year 2026 versus the consensus of $6.26 and $11 in 2027 compared to the $7.37 consensus.

The analysts also pointed out that token demand has skyrocketed in the last few months, which is increasing profit per token.

"OpenAI and other platforms are now generating 50-70% gross margins, leading to every stood up GPU now sold out," Muse said. "Customers are searching desperately for compute wherever they can find it. More specifically, in the last 12-16 weeks we have seen an inflection - likely tied to time-based reasoning explosion coupled with multimodal inputs, led by video where context length is super long."

Cantor is also convinced the rapid, wide-scale adoption of generative AI proves this is not a bubble.

"In the last 12 months, recommenders have adopted Generative AI," Muse noted. "Search has moved to Generative AI. Social Media to Generative AI. User-Generated content, Ad recommendation, everything has moved from classical machine learning to Generative AI. NVDA sees $2 Trillion in spending here from traditional compute to Generative AI alone. We are simply not in a bubble, rather the market is beginning to see how 'quality' AI can deliver meaningfully positive ROI's."

Cantor believes the AI infrastructure market will range from $3T to $4T by 2030.

data.title: Intel unveils next-gen processor built on its new semiconductor process 18A
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) unveiled architectural details for its next generation client processor Intel Core Ultra series 3, code-named Panther Lake.

Shares of Intel rose about 1% on Thursday.

Intel said Panther Lake is expected to start shipping later this year and is the company’s first product built on Intel 18A, the most advanced semiconductor process ever developed and manufactured in the U.S.

The company also previewed Xeon 6+ (code-named Clearwater Forest), its first Intel 18A-based server processor, which is expected to launch in the first half of 2026.

Both Panther Lake and Clearwater Forest, as well as multiple generations of products built on Intel 18A, are being manufactured at Fab 52, Intel’s new, state-of-the-art factory in Chandler, Arizona, the company said in a press release on Thursday.

The company added that Arizona’s Fab 52 is fully operational and set to reach high-volume production using Intel 18A later this year.

data.title: Wall Street ticks up as Powell takes a pass on policy outlook
data.description:
The stock market was slightly higher on Thursday as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell did not comment on the economic outlook or monetary policy at a Fed event. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.1%, the Dow (DJI) +0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.1%.

Wall Street’s major averages ended higher on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 tagging new record closes, all while gold (XAUUSD:CUR) topped $4,000/oz and FOMC minutes landed.

Silver also seems to be having its moment, as spot silver hit a historic $50/oz as investors piled into precious metals.

"Markets recovered their poise yesterday, with global equities at new highs as investors remained unfazed by the ongoing political uncertainty in various countries. So the S&P 500 (+0.58%) and the Nasdaq (+1.12%) both hit an all-time high, despite the government shutdown," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

There are no economic data slated on Thursday owing to the ongoing U.S. government shutdown.

"Over in the U.S., the focus remained on the government shutdown, amidst mounting speculation that it might even drag on to next month. That came as Republicans and Democrats showed no sign of publicly shifting their positions, with Democratic Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer saying that their position on healthcare subsidies remained the same," Reid added.

However, traders can tune into some speeches from Federal Reserve members throughout the day. 

Fed Chair Jerome Powell gave welcome remarks before the Community Bank Conference, however, he did not mention anything about the monetary policy or the economy. Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will also give opening remarks at the same event. 

Fed Board Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly will speak at separate events. 

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day. 

"There was a discussion about the Fed’s balance sheet—relevant as quantitative policy might make a difference to the U.S. government’s borrowing costs. Overall, there is nothing to dissuade investors from expecting further U.S. cuts, although the conviction of market views might lessen slightly," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

On the political side, Israel and Hamas have agreed to a ceasefire and hostage deal as part of the first phase of a peace plan advanced by U.S. President Donald Trump to end the war in Gaza.

Market Snapshot – October 9, 2025 (as of 9:30 a.m. ET)

data.title: Bullish sentiment inches higher, neutral sentiment declines in latest AAII survey
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors rose to 45.9% for the week ending October 8, 2025, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII). This marks an increase from the previous week’s 42.9%, reflecting growing optimism among investors.

Conversely, bearish sentiment declined slightly to 35.6%, down from 39.2% in the prior week, suggesting that fewer investors are expressing concerns about a market downturn. However, caution remains prevalent, with a significant portion of investors adopting a neutral outlook.

Silver prices neared $50 per ounce as investors moved into precious metals amid growing economic uncertainty. The surge in silver, along with a rise in gold prices above $4,000, came as concerns over a potential U.S. government shutdown and global growth issues pushed investors toward safer assets.

Meanwhile, U.S. stock indices like the Nasdaq and S&P 500 hit record highs, driven by strength in tech stocks. Despite the gains, concerns about inflation and global trade imbalances continued to weigh on sentiment, limiting broader market optimism.

Neutral sentiment, which indicates expectations for little change in stock prices over the next six months, fell to 18.5%, down from 19.1% the previous week. This indicates a shift toward more polarized views, with fewer investors holding a wait-and-see stance.

The survey, conducted by the AAII since 1987, continues to provide key insights into retail investor sentiment and how individual investors are positioning themselves for market conditions in the near term.
